mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200k signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142371k as of 2015-09-01 indicates sustained hiring
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where month-over-month PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.50% after a prolonged near-zero period, it signals the Fed's confidence in economic strength but also increases discount rates on future cash flows. This creates a negative pressure on equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% after 24 months below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2015-09-01 indicates near-zero rates still in effect
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and construction demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity markets respond positively within 20 trading days due to expectations of higher corporate revenues and capital expenditure.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447686M and HOUST at 1126k as of 2015-08-01 show concurrent strength
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of co-growth instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to predictive power (measured by incremental R²) is less than 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signal deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. This constrains corporate profit margins and signals recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice tail risk.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers a 60-day negative equity return as investors anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of 2015-09-01 indicates relatively tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary onset
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real rate environment. This signals monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.566 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2015-09-01
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term's incremental R² contribution is less than 0.008.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling weakening aggregate demand. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors downgrade earnings expectations and reprrice growth multiples.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates month-over-month by more than 0.8% while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously declines, indicating synchronized supply expansion and labor market tightening. This dual signal of capacity growth and full employment supports equity valuations over 20 trading days through higher expected corporate output and pricing power.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.8% AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.0793 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of 2015-08-01
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 57% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term's incremental R² is less than 0.009 over any 2-year rolling window.
